title: When will you start to turn a profit？
filename: When will you start to turn a profit？.en.vtt
excerpt: When will you start to turn a profit so this depends a lot on your strategy particularly your pricing strategy if you decide to go cheap to get experience so that you can attract more people which doesn't always follow that way but if you did you may never be profitable you have …

segments.text: when will you start to turn a profit so this depends a lot on your strategy particularly your pricing strategy if you decide to go cheap to get experience so that you can attract more people which doesn't always follow that way but if you did you may never be profitable you have to charge enough to make a profit to start with that's the crux point of when you'll make a profit is how much are you charging and what are your costs it is possible in the uk to train for between one to three thousand pounds would give you a good starting point the most important thing is you should be making gross profit from the start now gross profit is simply covering your costs and having some left over it doesn't mean that at the end of the year you're gonna have lots of money building up in the bank account because you will still need to reinvest a lot of that into other goods and services that you need to grow your practice but i would make sure that when you first start if you do a treatment for someone there's some money left over that's your gross profit you can then use that to build your business if you don't price so that there's gross profit all the funds to build your business will have

to come from your other incomes which is a really bad way to start depending on what you want to build i can tell you that i did not take any money out of my business for a full three years when i first started and i saved about 30 000 pounds that enabled me to take the next leap up and to get a premises and employ staff i don't even think i was particularly fast at doing that but it was three years of not actually taking any money out of the business but i did make a profit that was used to take the next step may help you to think about profit in that way which is to fund the next step in your business

transcript: when will you start to turn a profit so this depends a lot on your strategy particularly your pricing strategy if you decide to go cheap to get experience so that you can attract more people which doesn't always follow that way but if you did you may never be profitable you have to charge enough to make a profit to start with that's the crux point of when you'll make a profit is how much are you charging and what are your costs it is possible in the uk to train for between one to three thousand pounds would give you a good starting point the most important thing is you should be making gross profit from the start now gross profit is simply covering your costs and having some left over it doesn't mean that at the end of the year you're gonna have lots of money building up in the bank account because you will still need to reinvest a lot of that into other goods and services that you need to grow your practice but i would make sure that when you first start if you do a treatment for someone there's some money left over that's your gross profit you can then use that to build your business if you don't price so that there's gross profit all the funds to build your business will have to come from your other incomes which is a really bad way to start depending on what you want to build i can tell you that i did not take any money out of my business for a full three years when i first started and i saved about 30 000 pounds that enabled me to take the next leap up and to get a premises and employ staff i don't even think i was particularly fast at doing that but it was three years of not actually taking any money out of the business but i did make a profit that was used to take the next step may help you to think about profit in that way which is to fund the next step in your business
